methods.description: This method measures airway responsiveness in mice after exposure to birch pollen, using a Buxco FinePointe System for noninvasive airway measurement. It assesses the specific airway resistance (sRAW) after exposure to increasing concentrations of methacholine.

steps.description: Mice were given 5 min to acclimate and then exposed to aerosolized PBS to set a baseline value.
steps.searchable_summary: acclimate mice and set baseline with aerosolized PBS

steps.description: Mice were exposed to increasing concentrations of 100 μl aerosolized methacholine (3.125, 6.25, 12.5, 25, and 50 mg/mL in PBS for 1 min).
steps.searchable_summary: expose mice to increasing concentrations of aerosolized methacholine

steps.description: The specific airway resistance (sRAW) were measured after each methacholine aerosol application by the noninvasive airway device.

methods.description: This method involves the preparation and staining of lung tissues for histological analysis to assess inflammation and mucus production.

steps.description: Mice lungs were fixed with 10% formaldehyde and embedded in paraffin.
steps.searchable_summary: fix and embed lung tissues in paraffin

steps.description: Lung tissues were then cut into micro-slices, and stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E) or Alcian blue-periodic acid-Schiff (AB-PAS) for histological analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: cut and stain lung tissue slices for histological analysis

steps.description: Inflammatory scores and mucus scores were graded (0 = no inflammation to 4) as past studies described.
The membrane was treated with 5% skim milk for 2 h to block nonspecific binding and incubated with the mice serum pool overnight at 4°C.
steps.searchable_summary: block nonspecific binding and incubate with serum

steps.description: The membrane was incubated with 1:1000 the second antibodies rat antimouse IgE (HRP) for 1 h at room temperature.

methods.description: This method outlines the allergen sensitization and challenge protocol for BALB/c mice to study airway hyper-responsiveness.

steps.description: BALB/c mice were sensitized subcutaneously on days 1, 8, and 15 with 25 μg/μL birch pollen extracts, which contained 5 μg Bet v 1, absorbed to 100 μL alum.
steps.searchable_summary: sensitize mice with birch pollen extracts and alum

steps.description: On days 24-26, the mice were challenged with 0.1% birch pollen extract aerosol.
steps.searchable_summary: challenge mice with birch pollen aerosol

steps.description: After 1 day, airway hyper-responsiveness (AHR) to methacholine was assessed.
The control group were sensitized subcutaneously with Phosphate Buffered Saline (PBS) and challenged with PBS.
steps.searchable_summary: sensitize and challenge control group with PBS

methods.description: This method describes the development of a birch pollen-induced asthma model in BALB/c mice, including cytokine analysis and histological examination.

steps.description: BPE sensitized and challenged BALB/c mice developed AHR, especially at methacholine concentrations of 25 mg/mL and 50 mg/mL.
steps.searchable_summary: develop AHR model in mice with BPE

steps.description: In BALF, Th2-related cytokines IL-4, IL-5, and IL-6; Th17-related cytokines IL-17; and Treg-related IL-10 and TGF-β were significantly higher in the asthma group than in the control group.
Mice sensitized by BPE demonstrated increased plasma levels of anti-BPE IgE, IgG1, and IgG2a.
steps.searchable_summary: measure plasma levels of anti-BPE antibodies

steps.description: Histologic analyses showed that asthma mice lung had peribronchial infiltration of inflammatory cells and mucosal hyperplasia.
steps.searchable_summary: perform histological analysis on asthma mice lungs
